channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: This messaging mecca integrates Slack, WhatsApp, and Telegram
item.description: I like chatting with my friends—who doesn’t?—but I don’t always know where to find them. There are simply too many apps. Some of my friends text, others use WhatsApp, while others yet insist on using Discord or the DM feature in whatever random social network they prefer.
item.dc:creator: Justin Pot, Fast Company

item.title: Music could help ease pain from surgery or illness. Scientists are listening
item.description: Hospitals and doctors' offices are inviting singers and musicians to help patients manage their pain, as music's ability to reduce pain is gaining attention.
item.dc:creator: Christina Larson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: San Jose employees, meet your new chatbot assistant? City eyes expansion of AI
item.description: San Jose is soliciting proposals for a generative AI platform that can reduce some of the repetitive and administrative aspects of city workers' jobs.
item.dc:creator: Devan Patel, The Mercury News

item.title: How to watch New Zealand v England T20 series 2025 for FREE: live streams, schedule, teams
item.description: We'll show you all the ways you can watch New Zealand v England T20 live streams from anywhere in the world and potentially for free.
item.dc:creator: Roderick Easdale, TechRadar

item.title: Sex could become the next big business opportunity for AI companies
item.description: ChatGPT will be able to have kinkier conversations after OpenAI CEO Sam Altman announced the artificial intelligence company will soon allow its chatbot to engage in “erotica for verified adults.” OpenAI won’t be the first to try to profit from sexualized AI.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: The Wildlife Photographer of the Year 2025 awards were dominated by one pro camera – and it isn’t the one you might expect
item.description: Wildlife Photographer Of The Year 2025 finalists chose this one standout Canon camera the most, and it's not even a flagship model
item.dc:creator: Peter Fenech, TechRadar

item.title: Salesforce chief says he no longer backs Trump sending National Guard to San Francisco
item.description: Salesforce CEO Marc Benioff apologized Friday for comments suggesting that President Trump should deploy the National Guard to San Francisco, saying he no longer believes it is necessary.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: What is the 'AI homeless man prank'? Police says it's dangerous
item.description: The prank uses artificial intelligence to create a picture or video of a "homeless man" entering a person's home, going through their fridge, or lying in their bed.
item.dc:creator: Kyle Mitchell, NewsNation

item.title: Google is rolling out an unexpected update for Nest Mini and Audio smart speakers – and it’s all thanks to Gemini
item.description: An update is giving existing Google Nest speakers some new colored lights, reminiscent of Gemini's branding.
item.dc:creator: Rowan Davies, TechRadar

item.title: How to watch Tracker season 3 online
item.description: Colter Shaw's survival skills still crack cases, but is his family at the center of one of them? Here's how to watch Tracker season 3 online and from anywhere.
item.dc:creator: Andy Murray, TechRadar

item.title: How to watch the Orionid meteor shower, debris of Halley's comet
item.description: The Orionid meteor shower peaks this year with the arrival of a new moon, offering a rare chance to see shooting stars without moonlight interference.
item.dc:creator: Christina Larson, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: How to watch United States Grand Prix 2025 F1: free streams, Sprint weekend, schedule, Verstappen ahead of Norris on pole for Sprint on Saturday
item.description: All the ways to watch US Grand Prix live streams online and from anywhere, as title rivals Oscar Piastri, Lando Norris and Max Verstappen head to Austin, Texas.
item.dc:creator: Andy Murray, TechRadar

item.title: Australia sharing tips on curbing social media for children before age limit starts in December
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — The Australian government has begun a public education campaign with tips on how to wean children off social media ahead of a world-first national 16-year age limit taking effect in December.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Big Tech is paying millions to train teachers on AI, in a push to bring chatbots into classrooms
item.description: The country’s largest teachers unions are teaming up with tech companies like Microsoft and OpenAI to help educators build literacy in artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: Jocelyn Gecker, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Uber wants to become the ‘Uber’ of data labeling
item.description: Following its entrance into the data labeling space last year with the launch of a new division, Scaled Solutions, Uber on Thursday introduced a pilot project in the US market that will see its drivers, like their counterparts in India, annotate data for use in AI training.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Meta details cutting-edge networking technologies for AI infrastructure
item.description: Meta shared details about its AI and networking advances at this week’s 2025 OCP Global Summit in San Jose, Calif.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Major companies, including Google and Dior, hit by massive Salesforce data breach
item.description: Google, Dior, Allianz and dozens of other companies lost sensitive customer data in Salesforce-related breaches affecting millions of records across multiple sectors.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Energy Department offers $1.6 billion loan guarantee to upgrade transmission lines across Midwest
item.description: The Department of Energy says it has finalized a $1.6 billion loan guarantee to a subsidiary of one of the nation’s largest power companies to upgrade nearly 5,000 miles of transmission lines across five states, mostly in the Midwest, for largely fossil fuel-run energy.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Daly, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Anthropic releases new version of its smaller Haiku model
item.description: Anthropic is launching Claude Haiku 4.5, the newest version of the generative AI ​(genAI) ​company’s smallest language model. The company says it offers the same performance as Sonnet 4, but at a third of the cost and more than twice the speed.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Apple adds game-changing iPhone document feature that users waited years to see
item.description: Apple launches iOS 26 with new Preview app that combines document editing, PDF annotation and scanning features into one streamlined iPhone experience.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Security experts warn of increased cyber risk after end of Windows 10 support
item.description: Cyber security experts are warning Windows 10 users of an increased risk of malicious activity after support officially ended Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Brady Doran, NewsNation

item.title: Microsoft: Russia, China increasingly using AI to escalate cyberattacks on the US
item.description: Russia, China, Iran and North Korea are using artificial intelligence to improve their cyberattacks on U.S. companies, governments and individuals, according to new research from Microsoft.
item.dc:creator: David Klepper, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: 14 advanced Android 16 tips
item.description: Well, this is highly irregular. For the first time in many moons now, Google’s giving us not one but two new full-fledged Android versions in a single year. Android 16 officially arrived into the world this summer, and it’ll soon be followed by another release this fall.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Top Army official using ChatGPT to make military decisions: Report
item.description: An expert warned that, no matter how fast the tech can give an answer, nothing can replace human judgment when it comes to military calls.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: Apple’s Vision Pro: the Newton of the XR age
item.description: Apple’s alleged decision to suspend development of a lighter and cheaper Vision Pro device sounds a lot worse than it actually is, because in a short time the product will be seen as the Newton of the AR age. Why the Newton? Vision Pro is ahead of its time.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Using Valkey on Azure and in .NET Aspire
item.description: Big changes to the license used by the popular open source key/value store Redis prompted a fork, with the launch of Valkey. In the time since that fork in March 2024, the two projects have diverged.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Goodbye, SEO. Hello, GEO
item.description: When you type a question into an AI search engine like ChatGPT or Google AI Mode and it comes up with an answer, that information comes from somewhere.
item.dc:creator: Pete Pachal, Fast Company

item.title: Senate Democrats press Google, YouTube on Trump settlement
item.description: Several Senate Democrats are pressing Google and YouTube for answers about their decision to settle a lawsuit with President Trump over YouTube’s suspension of his account in the wake of the Jan. 6, 2021, attack on the U.S. Capitol. In a Wednesday letter to Google and YouTube executives, Sens.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: IBM unveils advanced quantum computer in Spain
item.description: The Guipuzcoan city of San Sebastián is now operating the IBM Quantum System Two quantum computer, the most advanced of its kind that IBM has developed commercially to date.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Q&A: IBM’s Mikel Díez on hybridizing quantum and classical computing
item.description: The Basque city of San Sebastián is beginning to play an interesting leading role in the radically different field of quantum technology. The official launch of IBM Quantum System Two, the most advanced quantum computer of the ‘Blue Giant,’ happened in Donostia-San Sebastián.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: DOGE’s arrival at Social Security sparked chaos and missed a chance for real reform
item.description: "There's a real brain drain that's happened there that I think they're still grappling with," said Eli Hager. The post DOGE’s arrival at Social Security sparked chaos and missed a chance for real reform first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Terry Gerton, Federal News Network

item.title: Researchers create revolutionary AI fabric that predicts road damage before it happens
item.description: New AI road monitoring system uses sensor-embedded fabric to predict infrastructure problems, potentially reducing maintenance costs and traffic disruptions for cities.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Nvidia’s DGX Spark desktop supercomputer is on sale now, but hard to find
item.description: Nvidia’s “personal AI supercomputer,” the DGX Spark, may run fast but it’s been slow getting here. It finally went on sale on Oct.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Instagram says it's safeguarding teens by limiting them to PG-13 content
item.description: Teenagers on Instagram will be restricted to seeing PG-13 content by default and won't be able to change their settings without a parent's permission, Meta announced on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, The Hill

item.title: It's called automated officiating. The NBA is utilizing it to get even more calls right
item.description: The play, in real time to the naked eye, might have looked very close to a violation. LeBron James leaped, got his right hand on the ball with a few tenths of the game’s final second remaining and tapped it through the basket to give the Los Angeles Lakers a buzzer-beating win last season.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: How to spot and stop AI phishing scams
item.description: AI phishing scams now use voice cloning and deepfake technology to trick victims, but Kurt "CyberGuy" Knutsson reveals warning signs to watch for.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
